LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CHABOT VETERINARY CLINIC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VETERINARY MALPRACTICE AND FOR ASSAULT, BATTERY, OR INJURY TO AN INDIVIDUAL ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATIONS. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Continued To Jun-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CHABOT VETERINARY CLINIC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's causes of action for veterinary malpractice and for assault, battery, or injury to an individual are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. Defendant's motion to strike and motion to require that undertaking be posted are denied in light of defendant's sustained demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CHABOT VETERINARY CLINIC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied in light of defendant's sustained demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require That Undertaking Be Posted By Out-Of-State Plaintiff
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CHABOT VETERINARY CLINIC'S Motion To Require That Undertaking Be Posted By Out-Of-State Plaintiff - Defendant's motion to require that undertaking be posted is denied in light of defendant's sustained demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
